channel.title: GitHub Copilot Bulletin — English / Biweekly / Admins
channel.description: Latest GitHub Copilot updates for Admins (Biweekly, English)

item.title: Copilot Bulletin Biweekly #3 — April 1 to April 15, 2026 · Admins Edition
item.description: The past two weeks mark a governance inflection point for enterprise admins: data residency and FedRAMP compliance arrive for Copilot, alongside new organization-level controls for the cloud agent—centralizing runner and firewall policy enforcement. Usage metrics also deepen, offering clearer visibility into how teams actively engage with code review and the agent across your organization.

item.title: Copilot Bulletin Biweekly #2 — March 23 to April 6, 2026 · Admins Edition
item.description: The past two weeks mark a clear turning point for Copilot governance at scale. GitHub has moved several organization-level controls from preview to general availability, and introduced new mechanisms for managing the cloud agent's security posture, runner infrastructure, and usage visibility across the entire organization — all configurable from a single place.

item.title: Copilot Bulletin Biweekly #1 — March 2 to March 16, 2026 · Admins Edition
item.description: The past two weeks focused on giving administrators stronger control over the expanding agentic ecosystem. From enterprise-wide session monitoring with new filters to repository-level workflow approval settings and agent hooks for policy enforcement, GitHub is building the governance layer admins need to manage AI agents at scale while balancing security with developer velocity.
